text: assets/validate.mjs enforces 'exactly one data-verdict' by matching /data-verdict="..."/g across the WHOLE file, which ALSO matches the six `.verdict[data-verdict="GO|GO-WITH-CONDITIONS|NO-GO"]{...}` selectors in the template's <style> block. So a real report rendered from assets/template.html (which keeps the full <style> per workflow step 8, 'Keep the <style> block unchanged') fails its own validator with 'expected exactly one data-verdict, found 7' — i.e. workflow step 9 ('run validate.mjs; must exit 0') can never pass on a genuine full report. The shipped evals/fixtures/*.html dodge this by being minimal (no <style>), so the floor never caught it. Fix validate.mjs to scope the verdict/contract checks to the document <body> (or strip the <style> block before matching, or match real element attributes rather than CSS attribute selectors). Add a fixture that is a COMPLETE template render (full <style> retained, placeholders filled) and assert it validates exit 0 — the discrimination the current minimal fixtures lack.

Expected: every plan-check report embeds exactly one <script type="application/json" id="dod"> block whose JSON is {"criteria":[{"id","text","tier":"checkable"|"judged","check"?}]} (check required iff tier is checkable), and assets/validate.mjs enforces it as contract item 6: missing block, duplicate blocks, malformed JSON, empty criteria array, bad tier, and checkable-without-check all exit 1; the success line reports the DoD criteria count. This is the machine-readable DoD that lirbox:conductor ingests as its --dod-file seed. Actual: reports carry no machine-readable DoD; conductor has nothing to ingest. SURFACE NOTE: the locked floor fixture evals/fixtures/clean.html is prepped on the baseline to already carry a well-formed #dod block (inert to today's validator), so the floor stays green when the validator starts requiring it — this item's frozen acceptance check must carry ITS OWN missing-#dod and bad-tier fixtures rather than touching evals/floor/**. Exact implementation guidance: Task 5 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-10-dod-and-panel-review.md in the MAIN checkout /Users/liemlhd/Documents/git/Personal/lirbox (gitignored — read via absolute path, not the worktree).
suggestedCriterion: validate.mjs exits 1 on a report lacking the #dod block and on one whose criterion has tier 'maybe' or a checkable criterion without a check; exits 0 on a well-formed report carrying a valid #dod block.

text: Expected: assets/template.html carries a visible 'Definition of done' section (criteria refined to checkable/judged, mirroring the machine block) plus the <script type="application/json" id="dod"> block with a comment stating the shape and that conductor consumes it; references/blind-spot.md 'Any plan' checklist gains a 'No definition of done' entry (unverifiable success criteria => BLIND-SPOT-RISK; the report still derives and emits a DoD, but the plan's silence blocks a clean GO); SKILL.md step 8 instructs filling the DoD section AND its machine-readable twin (extract the plan's success criteria, refine to checkable = concrete command / judged = cited evidence, never drop plan-stated ACs, derive-and-flag when the plan stated none) and the Quality bar gains 'A DoD or no clean GO'. Actual: none of these exist. Exact text: Task 6 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-10-dod-and-panel-review.md in the MAIN checkout /Users/liemlhd/Documents/git/Personal/lirbox (gitignored — read via absolute path).
suggestedCriterion: grep-based: template.html contains id="dod" and a 'Definition of done' heading; blind-spot.md contains 'No definition of done'; SKILL.md mentions the #dod block and 'A DoD or no clean GO'; the template stays self-contained (no external resources) so the existing floor and copy-button checks remain green.
